title: Chef Is Just Throwing Random Words

content.problemStatement:
Chef once had a deep epiphany and ended up saying: Given a sequence of positive integers a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​, if you take each of its 2N2^N2N subsequences and write down the sum of elements of this subsequence, what will the bitwise OR of the written integers be?

Yes, you read it right, but can you solve it?
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the bitwise OR of sums of all subsequences.
